title: Sunscreen on Cotton
stainChemistry: Sunscreen is an oil or silicone emulsion + UV filters (avobenzone, oxybenzone) + mineral blockers (titanium dioxide, zinc oxide) + emulsifiers. The most important chemistry fact: AVOBENZONE REACTS WITH IRON in water to produce rust-colored permanent stains. Hard water + avobenzone + heat = orange-yellow yellow marks that are hard to remove. Chlorine bleach also reacts with sunscreen to create persistent yellowing. Cotton tolerates NSD, POG, IPA, 3% H₂O₂. Treatment is oil-strip first, then addressing any iron-reacted avobenzone residue.
whyThisWorks: NSD / Dawn pulls the oil-silicone emulsion and mineral particles. For iron-reacted avobenzone (orange-brown yellowing), a dilute acid (lemon juice / vinegar) converts iron to a soluble form. 3% H₂O₂ clears any residual color. The two absolute rules: NO chlorine bleach (makes yellowing worse), NO hot water on fresh sunscreen (sets avobenzone iron reaction).
defaultAssumption: Sunscreen stains are the summer-stain invisible problem: transfer happens on car seats, beach towels, chair covers without the user noticing until they come home and it's already oxidized. Fresh sunscreen on light-colored cotton = high-confidence DIY; aged / hot-washed-in sunscreen = stubborn, possible plateau.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Blot fresh sunscreen with a folded white towel. Press straight down. CRITICAL: do NOT reach for chlorine bleach on sunscreen — chlorine + avobenzone produces permanent yellow staining. Use Dawn + peroxide lane only.

Apply NSD, work with a soft bristle brush. NSD emulsifies the oil-silicone base and suspends mineral particles (titanium dioxide, zinc oxide). Rinse cool. Repeat if mineral white residue is still visible.
For sport / water-resistant sunscreens with heavy silicone content, an NSD-only pass may not fully emulsify. Tamp POG on a white cloth from the back. Follow with NSD to clear POG residue.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dilute acetic acid 5% aqueous OR lemon juice
spottingProtocol.instruction: If an orange / rust-colored shadow remains (iron-reacted avobenzone — common on hard-water laundry), dab dilute acid on the shadow. Converts iron-complex to soluble form. Rinse cool.
For residual yellow color on whites: 3% H₂O₂. Dwell 3–5 min, blot. Skip on dark cotton. NEVER use chlorine bleach instead.
Launder warm per care label — NOT hot. Hot water can set the iron-avobenzone reaction if any oil residue remains. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.

steps: 1. Blot bulk. NO chlorine bleach — makes yellowing worse.
2. NSD — strip oil emulsion + mineral particles.
3. POG if heavy silicone sport formulation.
4. Dilute acetic acid for iron-reacted avobenzone yellowing.
6. Warm wash per care label (not hot).
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool through spotting; warm (not hot) wash post-chemistry
warnings: NO chlorine bleach — makes sunscreen yellowing permanent
Silicone-heavy sport sunscreens need POG, not just NSD

homeSolutions.instruction: The single worst thing for sunscreen stains is chlorine bleach. Bleach reacts with avobenzone (the main UV filter in most sunscreens) to produce a PERMANENT yellow stain. If you've already bleached the spot, don't bleach it again — move on to the acid + peroxide path.
Press a folded white towel or paper towels on the stain. Lift. Press dry section again. Get as much surface sunscreen off BEFORE wetting anything.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn + Cool Water — Soak the Oil Out
homeSolutions.instruction: Work a generous amount of Dawn dish soap directly into the stain with a soft toothbrush or fingertip. Let sit 10 minutes. Rinse cool. Repeat if you can still feel oiliness or see white residue (mineral sunscreen).

homeSolutions.agent: Lemon Juice Dab for Orange Yellow
homeSolutions.instruction: Sometimes a rust-orange shadow shows up after the Dawn pass — that's the avobenzone reacting with iron in your water. Dab a few drops of lemon juice or diluted white vinegar on it, wait 3 min, rinse cool. The acid turns the iron complex into a soluble form.
For stubborn yellow on white cotton: dab 3% drugstore hydrogen peroxide, 3–5 min, blot. Skip on dark cotton.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia.

homeSolutions.instruction: Launder WARM per care label — not hot. Hot water sets the iron-avobenzone reaction if any sunscreen remains. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.

If yellow persists or was bleach-set, take to a dry cleaner. Tell them: "Sunscreen on cotton, age [fresh / summer-washed-in], home-treated with Dawn + [lemon juice / 3% peroxide]. NO chlorine bleach used [/ bleach was applied before I knew]."

steps: 1. NO chlorine bleach — makes sunscreen yellowing permanent.
3. Generous Dawn + cool water, 10 min dwell, rinse. Repeat.
4. Lemon juice / vinegar dab for rust-orange shadow (iron-avobenzone).
6. WARM wash per care label — not hot.
7. Escalate if yellow persists or bleach was applied.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if chlorine bleach was applied (may be permanent), if hot water has set the reaction, or if two home rounds plateau.
warnings: WARM wash, not hot — hot water sets the iron reaction
Silicone sport sunscreens are tougher — may need solvent
Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia

neverDo: NEVER chlorine bleach on sunscreen — permanent yellow
Never hot water — sets iron-avobenzone reaction
Never dry with residual yellow — heat locks it
fiberSensitivities: Cotton tolerates NSD, POG, IPA, 3% H₂O₂, warm wash
Dyed cotton: skip peroxide or inside-seam-test
Heat-durable fiber; the avobenzone chemistry is the constraint

materialWarnings: NO bleach — chlorine + avobenzone = permanent yellow

escalation.when: Chlorine bleach applied (may be permanent), hot-dried into fiber, or two home rounds plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Sunscreen on cotton, age [fresh / summer-washed-in], home-treated with Dawn + [lemon juice / 3% H₂O₂]. [Bleach used or never / hot-dried or not]."

contextQuestions: Has any chlorine bleach touched this stain?
Regular sunscreen or silicone-based sport / water-resistant?
Mineral (zinc / titanium dioxide) or chemical (avobenzone) filter?
Has it been through a hot wash?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: The two absolute rules — no chlorine bleach, warm wash not hot — are what separates clean results from permanent yellowing. Iron-avobenzone orange shadow is the characteristic failure mode.
